original_abstract:
This research explores the development of social competence among high school students within the framework of non-formal education. Conducted between 2017 and 2019, the study involved an experimental group of 325 students and a control group of 322 students from seven secondary schools in the Ternopil and Ivano-Frankivsk regions of Ukraine. The authors define social competence through four primary structural dimensions: motivational, cognitive, activity-behavioural, and social-value. 

Initial diagnostic assessments revealed a significant deficiency in the social competence of prospective graduates, compounded by a lack of systematic teaching resources for non-formal learning in Ukraine. To address this, the researchers implemented specific socio-pedagogical conditions, including the stimulation of student subjectivity, the modernization of educational content through clubs and interest groups, and the provision of dedicated socio-pedagogical support. Post-experiment findings, verified using Student’s t-test and comparative correlation, demonstrated a substantial positive shift in the experimental group. Specifically, there was a 15% increase in students achieving a high level of social competence and an 11% reduction in those at the lowest level. The study concludes that shifting educational focus toward non-formal settings provides critical practical social skills and moral guidelines that formal education alone cannot fully deliver.
humanized_abstract:
This research deals with the formation of social competence of high school students in non-formal education. The experimental part of the research was implemented during 2017-2019 years. In the experiment, an experimental group consisted of 325 students and a control group consisted of 322 students from seven secondary schools of Ternopil and Ivano-Frankivsk regions of Ukraine. The social competence of high school students is considered in terms of four basic structural components: motivational, cognitive, activity-behavioural and social-value.

The initial diagnostic investigation has established that the target group of graduates lacks sufficient social competence, as well as effective learning materials and study aids for non-formal education in Ukraine. In order to resolve the identified challenges, the authors propose to introduce a set of socio-pedagogical conditions aimed at fostering students’ subjectivity, upgrading the educational material offered in the clubs of interests, and ensuring efficient socio-pedagogical assistance for graduates. The reliability of the experimental results obtained after the implementation of the suggested activities was verified by calculating Student’s t-test and applying the method of comparative correlation. In the experimental group, the social competence of graduates was steadily increased by 15 % and decreased by 11 % at the minimum level, respectively. The study confirms that educating graduates in non-formal settings will enable them to apply and apply the most essential social skills and moral standards in practice that cannot be acquired in a school formal educational setting.
